text.title: AC Mapping Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that hollow out AC-to-assertion mapping. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Structural-only assertions passed off as behavior assertions — assertion_class is filled out but asserted_behavior is a structural claim ("api was called").
failure-mode.detector: asserted_behavior matches the closed list of structural tokens OR test body contains only spy/mock/expect-called assertions with no state or output check
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer rejects the PR with a specific request: "add a behavior assertion that verifies the AC's effect (state, output, side-effect, or specific failure)". Engineer rewrites the test; reviewer re-walks the mapping.

failure-mode.description: Missing mapping — PR description has no mapping section, or the mapping is "TBD" / "see tests", evading the discipline.
failure-mode.detector: ac-mapping.yaml empty OR PR description lacks the mapping table OR mapping fields contain TBD / placeholder text
failure-mode.repair: CI rejects PR-ready transitions without a mapping. extract-acs.py auto-populates the AC list at PR open; engineer fills the rest. Pre-merge gate rejects PRs with empty mapping fields.

failure-mode.description: Wrong AC tagged — engineer maps an AC to a test that does not actually verify that AC (test exercises a different code path that happens to be in the same module).
failure-mode.detector: reviewer reads the test and finds the assertion does not match the AC text OR test name implies a different behavior than the mapping claims
failure-mode.repair: Reject the PR with the specific mismatch. Engineer either (a) writes a new test that actually verifies the AC and updates the mapping, or (b) restates the AC if it was the AC text that was unclear.

failure-mode.description: Mapping out of date — refactor renamed test files / test names, mapping file still points to old paths, validation fails.
failure-mode.detector: scripts/check-mapping-complete.py reports any tests[].file_path or test_name not found in the diff or main
failure-mode.repair: Run the script as a pre-merge gate. Engineer updates the mapping in the same PR. Long-term: introduce a stable test_id annotation (`@id ac-1-happy-path`) so renames do not break the mapping.

failure-mode.description: Reviewer rubber-stamps — reviewer skips the 5-minute walk, relies on CI green, structural-only assertions leak through.
failure-mode.detector: review approval comes within < 1 minute of PR-ready OR reviewer-effectiveness metric (assertion-rejection rate) trends to zero
failure-mode.repair: Track reviewer-effectiveness as a team metric. Re-train reviewers on the structural-vs-behavior distinction; pair reviewer with author for 1 week on a difficult PR to recalibrate.

failure-mode.description: AC drift — story's AC list changes mid-PR, mapping is not updated, merged PR claims to cover ACs that the story no longer contains.
failure-mode.detector: linked story's AC count differs from mapping AC count OR mapping references ac_id no longer in the story
failure-mode.repair: extract-acs.py re-runs at PR-ready and at pre-merge; diff between story ACs and mapping ACs triggers a review request. Story changes mid-PR are merged into the mapping explicitly.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI-assisted test generation output frequently labels every test with high-confidence claims of "behavior tested". The mapping's behavior-vs-structural classification is the discipline that prevents this from leaking. Reviewer trusts the test body and the assertion class, not the test name or the engineer's enthusiasm.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly: testing lead reviews a sample of past PRs and checks whether the mapping's asserted_behavior claims actually held. Mappings that overpromised (structural assertions described as behavioral) are flagged and used to recalibrate reviewer training.
